Title: Engineering Camelina sativa (L.) Crantz for enhanced oil and seed yields by combining diacylglycerol acyltransferase1 and glycerol-3-phosphate dehydrogenase expression

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final acylation step of TAG assembly
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the acyl-CoA-dependent conversion of DAG to TAG in the endoplasmic reticulum
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control/final-step enzyme in TAG assembly; rate-limiting not definitively established in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed carbon and energy storage; nutritionally and industrially important oil for biodiesel/green diesel and feed/food uses
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene highly expressed in developing seeds at 16–21 DAF under a seed-specific glycinin promoter
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Glasshouse growth under seed-specific transgene expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Seed-specific overexpression in Camelina; qRT-PCR confirmation; seed oil quantification by NMR; total oil yield calculation; FAME profiling by GC-FID; seed mass and yield measurements
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases seed oil content and seed mass; when co-expressed with ScGPD1 markedly increases seed yield, total oil yield, and oil harvest index
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Seed-specific AtDGAT1 overexpression in Camelina increased seed mass (~23% average) and seed oil content (~5.6% average, with some lines up to ~11%). 2. AtDGAT1 expression altered fatty acid composition, increasing C16:0 and C18:2 and decreasing C18:1, C18:3, and C20:1. 3. Co-expression with ScGPD1 produced the strongest phenotype, including up to ~52% higher seed mass, ~8–13% higher oil content, and about twofold higher oil yield/oil harvest index versus WT.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful TAG pathway engineering target for increasing Camelina seed oil and oil yield; especially valuable in gene stacking with precursor-supply genes such as GPD1 under seed-specific promoters
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered fatty acid composition; no significant change in seed protein content, germination, or early seedling growth under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors also tested a modified AtDGAT1S205A version; native AtDGAT1 + GPD1 gave the best overall performance among combinations tested.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Acyl-CoA:diacylglycerol acyltransferase, DGAT1 family; serine205-to-alanine mutant
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final acylation step of TAG assembly
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the acyl-CoA-dependent conversion of DAG to TAG; S205A mutation was designed to test enhanced activity
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key final-step enzyme variant; enhanced control strength versus native DGAT1 not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed carbon and energy storage; oil for biofuel and nutritional uses
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene highly expressed in developing seeds at 16–21 DAF under a seed-specific glycinin promoter
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Glasshouse growth under seed-specific transgene expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-directed mutant transgene overexpression in Camelina; qRT-PCR; seed oil quantification; seed mass and yield measurements; FAME analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases seed oil content and seed mass, but did not significantly outperform native AtDGAT1; with ScGPD1 also improved oil-related traits
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Seed-specific expression of AtDGAT1S205A increased seed oil content (~8.5% average) and seed mass relative to WT. 2. The S205A modification did not confer a significant advantage over native AtDGAT1 in seed yield, seed size, or overall growth. 3. Co-expression with ScGPD1 improved oil traits, but the strongest overall phenotype was observed with native AtDGAT1 + ScGPD1.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering variant of DGAT1, but this study does not support a clear advantage over native AtDGAT1 for Camelina improvement
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered fatty acid composition; no detected detrimental effects on seed protein, germination, or early seedling growth under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutation targeted a putative SnRK1 phosphorylation site (Ser205 to Ala), but authors note other phosphorylation sites may also affect AtDGAT1 activity.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Triacylglycerol (TAG) biosynthesis / glycerol backbone precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Glycerol-3-phosphate precursor supply for TAG assembly
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces DHAP to glycerol-3-phosphate, increasing glycerol backbone supply for TAG assembly
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as an important precursor-supply step; rate-limiting not definitively established in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed carbon and energy storage; oil for biodiesel/green diesel and nutritional applications
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene highly expressed in developing seeds at 16–21 DAF under a seed-specific oleosin promoter
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Glasshouse growth under seed-specific transgene expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Seed-specific overexpression in Camelina; qRT-PCR confirmation; seed oil quantification by NMR; FAME profiling; seed mass and yield measurements
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases seed mass and seed oil content; when co-expressed with AtDGAT1 gives synergistic increases in seed yield, oil yield, and oil harvest index
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Seed-specific ScGPD1 overexpression increased seed mass (up to ~30%) and seed oil content (average ~5.4%, some lines up to ~10–11%) relative to WT. 2. ScGPD1 expression altered fatty acid composition, increasing C16:0 and C18:2 while decreasing C18:1, C18:3, and C20:1. 3. Co-expression with AtDGAT1 gave the largest gains in seed mass, oil content, seed yield, and total oil yield, consistent with synergistic enhancement of TAG flux.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable precursor-supply engineering target for seed oil enhancement, particularly in stacking strategies with terminal TAG assembly enzymes such as DGAT1
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered fatty acid composition; no significant changes in seed protein content, germination, or early seedling growth under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a cytosolic G3P-supplying enzyme to enhance flux into TAG assembly.
